description_long: This lantern features a robust, angular post made of a reddish-brown material, likely metal, that bends at a right angle to support a hexagonal, yellowish lamp housing. The lamp housing appears to be made of a translucent material, possibly glass or plastic, designed to diffuse light evenly. The post is thick and sturdy, suggesting it is intended for outdoor use, such as along streets or in parks. The lamp housing is relatively compact compared to the post, with flat faces and slightly beveled edges. There are no visible decorative elements or intricate patterns, giving the lantern a utilitarian and modern appearance. The overall design prioritizes durability and functionality, suitable for urban environments.
description: A sturdy, angular post-mounted lantern with a hexagonal yellowish lamp housing and a modern, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness of the post and the narrow profile of the hexagonal lamp housing.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the right-angled bend of the post and the full face of the lamp housing.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the length and solidity of the post with the lamp housing partially visible.
description_view_3: Angled view from the other side, showing the lamp housing's flat face and the post's angular structure.
